reasoning: Provide a concise summary of the article’s key points: attendance decline after the move, plans to switch to Friday home games, Cotton’s remarks on improving fan experience and infrastructure, Cotton’s return and governance involvement, Diamond’s board seat and potential conflicts, and the transfer updates (new full-back likely, Nick Scott on trial).
output: Attendance at Sale has fallen since moving from Edgeley Park to the AJ Bell Stadium in 2012. The club plans to move home games back to Friday nights next season. Cotton says the early move delivered a poor fan experience (queues, motorway delays, trouble buying drinks) and that improvements to infrastructure are planned. The 69-year-old former player has returned after Simon Orange’s takeover and wants a strong north-of-England club, with directors now more regularly attending games. Steve Diamond will sit on Sale’s 10-man board, but Cotton says there will be no conflict of interest with a coach also serving as a director. Diamond also indicated they are close to signing a new full-back and are still looking for another backline player, with Nick Scott (a former Bath and London Welsh winger) on trial.
